title: cant make?
body:
[ec2-user@ip-10-250-192-14 podman]$ make BUILDTAGS=""
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag.         Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based      distro for journald support.
CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build \
        -mod=vendor  \
        -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.gitCommit=3d84661195aa8fbf3dab67375099cb991fd7fe81-dirty -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.buildInfo=1651637810 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc -X github.com/containers/common/pkg/config.additionalHelperBinariesDir= ' \
        -tags "" \
        -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
# github.com/proglottis/gpgme
vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:176:12: could not determine kind of name for C.gogpgme_data_seek
vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:176:38: could not determine kind of name for C.gpgme_off_t
cgo:
gcc errors for preamble:
In file included from vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:6:0:
./go_gpgme.h:15:8: error: unknown type name 'gpgme_off_t'
 extern gpgme_off_t gogpgme_data_seek(gpgme_data_t dh, gpgme_off_t offset, int whence);
        ^~~~~~~~~~~
./go_gpgme.h:15:55: error: unknown type name 'gpgme_off_t'; did you mean 'gpgme_key_t'?
 extern gpgme_off_t gogpgme_data_seek(gpgme_data_t dh, gpgme_off_t offset, int whence);
                                                       ^~~~~~~~~~~
                                                       gpgme_key_t

make: *** [bin/podman] Error 2

comments.body:
Please provide more details. What version are you trying to compile and on which distro?
Have you installed the dependencies listed here https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#build-and-run-dependencies?
Looks like this is an issue with compiling Podman 4.1.0 (possibly other versions as well) on Amazon Linux 2. I encountered the same problem just now.

It worked for me on a centos, just couldn’t make it work on amazon linux

> On 12 May 2022, at 1:13 pm, Michael S. Fischer ***@***.***> wrote:
> 
> 
> Looks like this is an issue with compiling Podman 4.1.0 (possibly other versions as well) on Amazon Linux 2. I encountered the same problem just now.
> 
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14105#issuecomment-1124485863>, or unsubscribe <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AF2GPP4KRFY6XMWXUZILHLLVJRZNRANCNFSM5VAYQTMA>.
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
>

It appears to be an underlying issue with the [Go gpgme bindings](http://github.com/proglottis/gpgme). Looks like it doesn't have backward compatibility with some older gpgme versions, such as the one that ships with AL2.

We can cannot reasonable support such old versions, including RHEL7 which seems to be the base for AL2.
You could try to use the `containers_image_openpgp` build tag to make it not use the c lib..
